title: Sideroxylon tepicense
taxonomy: Sideroxylon tepicense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. This plant is native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions of the continent. The genus Sideroxylon is a member of the order Ericales and the family Sapotaceae is part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Sideroxylon tepicense is further grouped into the subfamily Chrysophoroideae, which is a subgroup within the Sapotaceae family. The classification of Sideroxylon tepicense reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.

intro: Sideroxylon tepicense, also known as Guanacaste, is a tree species native to Central America, characterized by its distinctive bark and medicinal properties. The Guanacaste tree is valued for its potential health benefits, including the ability to treat gastrointestinal issues, fever, and respiratory problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sideroxylon tepicense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
